summary: Sprint 31 — ARENAKORE DNA Athletic Hub (6-axis radar, Advanced CRM table, DeepProfilePanel, Crew Panel). CRITICAL BUG FOUND & FIXED: athletes.tsx line 22 had `compare?: any` in TypeScript destructuring parameter — invalid TS/JS syntax causing Metro SyntaxError crash (entire app broken). Fixed by removing `?: any` (keeping `compare` without type annotation, already handled by the interface on the right side). After fix: backend 27/27 tests passed, frontend fully operational.

ui_bugs.issue: Mini radar (90px size) in CREW PANEL has overlapping axis labels at small size — cosmetic, functional

issues: Mini radar labels (END/POW/MOB/TEC/REC/AGI) overlap at 90px size in crew card — minor cosmetic issue

action_items: FIXED: athletes.tsx line 22 — `compare?: any` removed from destructuring pattern (was `compare?: any`, fixed to `compare`). This was a fatal SyntaxError crashing the app.

INFO: T.BUTLER and M.JORDAN show ATHLETE role badge in CREW PANEL (not COACH) — this is because they are not in the `coaches` array of the SHADOW SQUAD crew document (their platform role is COACH but crew membership role defaults to ATHLETE). No action needed unless crew roles should sync with platform roles.
INFO: SCAN column shows '—' for all athletes — no scan_results exist for gym athletes. Expected empty state.

INFO: 'DNA ATHLETIC HUB' explicit heading not rendered on page (the component title is ATLETI CRM in topbar tabs). The review request mentions 'shows DNA ATHLETIC HUB' — the page title in sidebar shows 'ATHLETE CRM' and top content shows 'ATLETI CRM | CREW PANEL'. If an explicit 'DNA ATHLETIC HUB' heading is required, consider adding it to the topBar.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: athletes.tsx line 22 — TypeScript destructuring parameter had `compare?: any` inside the destructuring braces `{ data, size = 180, showLabels = true, compare?: any }`. The `?:` syntax is only valid in type declarations/interfaces, NOT in destructuring patterns. This caused Metro bundler SyntaxError crash blocking the entire app. Fix: remove `?: any` to just `compare` — the type annotation `compare?: Record<string, number>` on the right side handles the optional typing.
MINOR: No testID attributes on the athlete table rows or crew panel elements — makes future automated testing harder. Consider adding testID to athlete rows and crew cards.
MINOR: athletes.tsx is 758 lines — at the boundary of the 700-line guideline for split recommendation.
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/coach-studio/athletes.tsx (line 22: removed `?: any` from SixAxisRadar destructuring parameter)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 31 DNA Athletic Hub is fully operational after fixing the TypeScript syntax error in athletes.tsx. Backend: all 5 new endpoints (/api/coach/athletes/full, /api/coach/athlete/{id}/full-profile, /api/crew/manage, /api/crew/invite, /api/crew/invitations/{id}/respond) return correct data structures with six_axis, injury_risk, global_rank, crews, scan_trend. Frontend: ATLETI CRM table shows rank/avatar/DNA/RISK badge/SCAN/CREW/global rank/mini radar columns correctly. Clicking athlete opens DeepProfilePanel (6-axis radar, axis breakdown, injury risk + progress bar, trend scan sparkline, multiskill editor). CREW PANEL shows SHADOW SQUAD with weighted DNA 73.8, avg six-axis bars, member list with OWNER/ATHLETE role badges, invite form. Filters (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW/TUTTI), sorts (DNA/RISCHIO/RANK/LVL), and search all working. Metro cache was cleared to pick up the fix.
rca_of_issue: CRITICAL BUG RCA: athletes.tsx line 22 `compare?: any` in destructuring — TypeScript only allows `?:` in interface/type declarations (right side of `:` in function signature). In a destructuring pattern `{ compare?: any }`, the `?` after the variable name is NOT valid JavaScript/TypeScript syntax. Babel (used by Metro bundler) raised SyntaxError: 'Unexpected token, expected ,' at position 22:68. Metro showed the error overlay immediately preventing any page from loading. Fix: change `{ data, size = 180, showLabels = true, compare?: any }` to `{ data, size = 180, showLabels = true, compare }` — the interface definition on the right side already declares `compare?: Record<string, number>` which is the valid location for the `?` optional marker.
